text.title: Two-Track Podcast Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Two-Track Podcast Strategy application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Plan MUST distinguish guest-appearance track (faster ROI) from owned-show track (long-term authority). Conflating them is rejected.
rule.rationale: The two tracks have different cadence, prep, and ROI horizons; treating them as one collapses both.

rule.statement: Owned show MUST record ≥3 episodes before public launch. Launching with episode 1 only is rejected.
rule.rationale: Algorithm placement collapses on schedule slips; a 3-episode buffer absorbs the first guest reschedule.

rule.statement: Show MUST have a niche positioning sentence naming audience + topic + angle. Generic 'business podcast' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Discovery algorithms favour niches; generic shows lose to keyword-tight shows.

rule.statement: Each guest appearance MUST be supported by a prep doc with talking points + plug position + CTA. Improv-only is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without prep, the plug or CTA gets dropped; the appearance becomes content for the host, not a growth move.

rule.statement: Every published episode MUST yield ≥3 derivative assets (clip + transcript + post). Single-format publishing is rejected.
rule.rationale: Audio-only effort produces single-channel reach; repurposing multiplies impressions per recording hour.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
